sections.paragraph: πτερύγιον, ου, τό [dim. of πτέρυξ] ‘a very high point in a building’, auditors of the gospels might well have recognized what was specifically signified in view of the reputation enjoyed by Herod’s temple, but the modern reader must be satisfied with approximation in imagery of a wing tip, high edge, summit Mt 4:5; Lk 4:9.
